events.label: assistant
events.text: Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`. Bottom line: a one-tooth timing mark offset is outside OEM tolerance — it's a direct crank-to-cam misalignment that risks piston-to-valve contact (interference engines) or misfire/poor running otherwise, so it's a **fail**, **critical** risk, requiring re-indexing the timing drive to OEM marks before any close-out or engine start.
